description: Seminary-wide configuration: branding, billing and enrollment windows, attendance and check-in rules, portal options, scholarships, and integrations. Set these once during setup and revisit when institutional policies change.

fields.description: Mirror Campuses, Buildings, and Rooms into the ERPNext Location tree so Assets can be placed in a room. Turn off if this seminary doesn't track Assets.
fields.label: Sync rooms to Asset Locations

fields.description: Optional. Root of the Location subtree the seminary's campuses/rooms hang under. Leave blank to auto-create and use a "Seminary Locations" root.
fields.label: Root Asset Location

fields.description: If enabled, field Academic Term will be Mandatory in Program Enrollment Tool.
fields.label: Make Academic Term Mandatory
Instructor Records to be created by
fields.options:
Full Name
Naming Series
Employee Number

fields.description: By default, the Sales Invoice Created will be in Draft Mode. If enabled, the Sales Invoice will be submitted once created.
fields.label: Submit Sales Invoice from Enrollments

fields.description: If enabled, the daily scheduler generates invoices on Academic Term start, Academic Year start, and the 1st of each month. Disable during initial setup or data migration.
fields.label: Enable Automated Billing

fields.description: If enabled, the daily scheduler advances Course Schedule states by their enrollment dates (Draft → Open for Enrollment, Open for Enrollment → Enrollment Closed) and sends late-grade reminders. Disable for seminaries that prefer fully manual control of the lifecycle.
fields.label: Auto-advance Course Schedule states

fields.description: Anchors and offsets used to compute Course Schedule enrollment dates seminary-wide. Anchors: term_start / term_end (from Academic Term); classes_start / classes_end (from each Course Schedule's start/end date). Offset is in days; negative = before anchor. Leave anchor blank to opt out of a window — Course Schedules will then need explicit overrides or land Open immediately. Individual Course Schedules can override these dates.
fields.label: Enrollment Window Rules

fields.description: Once you allow audits, you will need to create a Fee Category, Item, and Payer Fee Category PE to match this (in the later, select the Is Audit checkbox
fields.label: Allow audits?

fields.description: If not checked, the corresponding fee will be the same for all audited courses.
fields.label: Should audit be charged per credit hour?

fields.description: In this version, all students will advance, regardless of their performance. However, a manual submission will be required when these validations will occur.
fields.label: Auto-advance students in Time-based Programs?

fields.description: This customer will be used in all Sales Invoices of Scholarships.
fields.label: Scholarship - Customer

fields.description: Provide a small (100x100px), round image (or with enough blank space) to be used in the Student Portal. This is for the light theme,
fields.label: Seminary Logo (light theme)

fields.description: The app has a default instruction on how students and staff can import calendars to Google, Apple, Microsoft and Others. Any text here will completely override that description.
fields.label: Course Calendar Instructions
Show Student Application button on login

fields.description: Application form used when a Program does not specify its own. Leave blank to use the built-in form.
fields.label: Default Application Web Form

fields.description: This is the system user for portal support for students. Referenced by the "Support User" audience in Portal Messaging Rules below.
fields.label: Support User

fields.description: Who portal users may message from their Inbox, per sender role. Leave empty to fall back to the built-in defaults (students message their course instructors; staff message the whole roster).
fields.label: Portal Messaging Rules

fields.description: When checked, students will see the withdrawal dates for the course (defined per academic term) and have a button to request course withdrawal.
fields.label: Allow students to request course withdrawal on Portal

fields.description: Global gate for portal scholarship applications. A scholarship is shown/applicable on the portal only when this is on AND the scholarship's own "Show on Portal" is checked (double gate).
fields.label: Allow students to apply for scholarships on Portal

fields.description: Setting dates per term allows the system to automatically apply withdrawal rules, that can be overridden manually
fields.label: Set Withdrawal dates per Academic Term

fields.description: This setting will determine what needs to occur when there is a payment from the Scholarship Customer; all the specifics are handled by Withdrawal Rules
fields.label: Default Scholarship Procedure on Withdrawals
fields.options:
Refund to Scholarship Fund
Refund to Scholarship Fund and Create Invoice for Student

fields.description: When checked, students will have access to Enrollment page listing only courses available for their program, which they have not yet taken. They will be able to cancel the enrollment before it is submitted.
fields.label: Allow students to request course enrollment on Portal

fields.description: Provide a small (100x100px), round image (or with enough blank space) to be used in the Student Portal. This is for the dark theme,
fields.label: Seminary logo (dark theme)

fields.description: This requires the installation of another app "Payments" by Frappe. Payments makes the bridge between this and the Payment Gateways.
fields.label: Enable Online Payments

fields.description: Optional HTML shown on the Student Applicant payment screen alongside (or instead of) the online gateway button. Use for bank transfer details, manual payment instructions, contact info for payment plans, etc.
fields.label: Alternative Payment Instructions

fields.description: This requires the installation of another app "HRMS" by Frappe. HRMS enables payroll for salaried and per-course instructors via Salary Structure, Payroll Entry, and Salary Slip.
fields.label: Enable HRMS Payroll

fields.description: Controls when per-course instructor pay is released across Salary Slips. 'End of period' pays the full amount on the slip whose period contains the course's term end date. '50% at start + 50% at end' splits the pay between the term-start and term-end slips.
fields.label: Instructor Payment Split
fields.options:
End of period
50% at start + 50% at end

fields.description: Cutoff for HRMS-driven instructor pay. Only courses whose start date is on or after this date are considered. Leave blank to include all history. Use this to adopt HRMS mid-operation without sweeping legacy courses into the first payroll run.
fields.label: HRMS Live Date (Pay Cutoff)

fields.description: Master switch for syncing confirmed Chapel services to the official Google Calendar below. Off by default. When on, it also seeds the default of each new Chapel's own 'Sync with Google Calendar' checkbox (a chaplain can still override per service).
fields.label: Sync chapels with Google Calendar

fields.description: The shared institutional Google Calendar that official events (chapels, etc.) are pushed to. Configure a Google Calendar record first (Integrations > Google Calendar).
fields.label: Official Google Calendar

fields.description: How many minutes before a chapel's start time students may begin self check-in. Set both this and 'closes after' to 0 to disable the time window entirely (check-in allowed any time while the chapel is confirmed).
fields.label: Chapel Check-in Opens Before (mins)

fields.description: How many minutes after a chapel's end time (or start, if no end) students may still self check-in. Set both this and 'opens before' to 0 to disable the time window entirely.
fields.label: Chapel Check-in Closes After (mins)

fields.description: When checked, students must enter the chapel's human-readable check-in code (shown to the chaplain on the Chapel record, to display on screen during the service) to mark attendance.
fields.label: Require check-in code

fields.description: Lets students self-record course attendance from their course card ("Mark my attendance"). The instructor still reviews and finalizes the roster on the attendance page.
fields.label: Course Attendance Self Check-in

fields.description: When checked, self check-in is time-based: the system auto-selects the meeting happening now (within the window below) and optionally requires a code — best when your site Time Zone (System Settings) matches the seminary. When unchecked, students instead pick any past meeting they have no attendance for (no clock dependency).
fields.label: Enforce meeting time window

fields.description: When checked, students must enter the meeting's human-readable check-in code to self-record course attendance. The code is generated on demand from the attendance page ("Show check-in code").
fields.label: Require check-in code

fields.description: How many minutes before a meeting's start time students may begin self check-in.
fields.label: Check-in Opens Before (mins)

fields.description: How many minutes after a meeting's end time (or start, if no end) students may still self check-in.
fields.label: Check-in Closes After (mins)

fields.description: Grace period after a meeting's start time. A self check-in later than this is recorded as Tardy instead of Present. Set to 0 to never auto-mark Tardy on self check-in.
fields.label: Tardy After (mins)

fields.description: When checked, online meetings are attendance-bearing (students can check in / instructors mark them, and they count toward each course's scheduled-meeting total). Uncheck to make online meetings calendar-only — excluded from check-in, instructor marking, and the absence-limit math. (A fully online section can instead set its Attendance Policy to Disabled.)
fields.label: Track Attendance for Online Meetings

fields.description: Seminary-wide attendance-failure policy. The per-course absence limit is auto-derived from each Program's max absence % × the course's scheduled meetings, overridable per Course Schedule by the registrar.
fields.label: Attendance Policy

fields.description: How many Tardies count as one Absence toward the limit (e.g. 3 → every 3 tardies add 1 absence). Set to 0 to disable tardy-to-absence conversion.
fields.label: Tardies per Absence

fields.description: Warn (student banner + notification) when a student is within this many absences of the limit. Crossing the limit triggers a second, stronger notification.
fields.label: Warn Within (absences of limit)

fields.description: Set a default letter head for student facing documents (audit reports, etc.)
fields.label: Default Letter Head

fields.description: When checked, on each course there will be actions to report disciplinary incidents, at the course and assessment levels. A second gate on the disciplinary reason applies, so only when both conditions are met such type of reporting can occur.
fields.label: Instructors create Disciplinary Incident

name: Seminary Settings
